Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1 [read/lookup]: Search email correspondence related to Destiny Pham, the $14,000 deposit, the Facebook Marketplace / furniture-sale explanation, and loan LN-2026-00610 using search_emails to identify what explanation has already been provided, what documentation has already been requested, and whether any corroborating support was previously mentioned or sent in the thread.
OE 2 [read/lookup]: Search internal processing discussion related to Destiny Pham, the $14,000 deposit, and LN-2026-00610 using conversations_search_messages to identify the relevant Slack messages in #loan-processing (C002) about the $14K deposit on LN-2026-00610, noting the channel_id and the timestamp of the most relevant message for replying, the current team understanding, and the next agreed follow-up step.
OE 3 [read/lookup]: Look up loan LN-2026-00610 in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan or mortgage_los_search_loans to confirm the current loan record, assigned parties, rate lock expiration date, and whether any conditions or document items exist related to deposit sourcing (cross-referencing with email/Slack findings to establish what's outstanding). Optionally use mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status on the loan to verify the lock timeline status.
OE 4 [read/lookup]: Review CRM records for Destiny Pham using crm_search_contacts and crm_list_engagements to identify prior outreach, internal notes, or borrower follow-up history related to the deposit explanation and requested support.
OE 5 [read/lookup]: Locate the borrower's linked financial account by searching for the Stripe customer using stripe_search_customers or stripe_list_fc_accounts, then review the borrower's bank transaction data using stripe_list_fc_transactions to confirm the presence, date, amount, and any available transaction detail for the $14,000 deposit that could help document the source of funds.
OE 6 [read/lookup]: Look up Destiny Pham's current contact details using contacts_search_contacts so the borrower follow-up is sent to the correct recipient.
OE 7 [write action]: Send a follow-up email to Destiny Pham using send_email that clearly requests the specific missing documentation or corroboration needed to support the $14,000 deposit for underwriting, without implying that the deposit has already been cleared.
OE 8 [write action]: Add a plain internal loan note to LN-2026-00610 using mortgage_los_add_activity with action set to note, summarizing what is already documented about the deposit explanation, what remains outstanding, the next follow-up step, and noting the rate lock timeline status if the lock is expiring or has already expired.
OE 9 [write action]: Reply in #loan-processing using conversations_add_message, using the discovered channel_id (C002) and the ts of a relevant message from OE 2 as the thread_ts to create a threaded reply, and summarize the current documentation status, the key missing support, the next operational step, and flag any rate lock timeline concern if applicable.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The borrower follow-up email explicitly asks for additional corroboration for the $14,000 Facebook Marketplace furniture-sale explanation.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly states the LOX alone is not sufficient and requests additional documentation specifically tied to the Facebook Marketplace furniture-sale explanation, including Marketplace screenshots, Messenger conversations with buyers, and an itemized breakdown.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states 'the lender's underwriting team will need additional supporting documentation before they can accept the sourcing on that deposit' and lists specific items needed to corroborate the Facebook Marketplace furniture-sale explanation.
✓Run #3The email explicitly requests corroborating documentation for the $14k Facebook Marketplace furniture-sale explanation, including FB Marketplace listing screenshots, Messenger records, photos, sale breakdown, and explanation for the single-deposit pattern.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states 'the lender's underwriting team will need additional supporting documentation before they can clear this item' and lists specific corroboration tied to the Facebook Marketplace explanation.
✓Run #5The email explicitly states the signed LOX is insufficient and requests 'supporting documentation that ties the deposit to the furniture sales you described,' directly referencing the Facebook Marketplace explanation.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly states the $14,000 deposit condition is unresolved and requests supporting documentation tied to the Facebook Marketplace furniture-sale explanation, specifically asking for Marketplace screenshots, Messenger conversations, photos, and a written sale breakdown.
3 The borrower follow-up email includes more specific examples of what corroboration would be acceptable (e. g., Marketplace screenshots, buyer message threads, itemized sale details, photos, receipts, or similar records).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email provides four specific examples of acceptable corroborating evidence: (1) Facebook Marketplace listing screenshots, (2) Facebook Messenger conversations with buyers, (3) a written itemized breakdown, and (4) photos of the furniture items.
✓Run #2The email provides highly specific examples: (1) Facebook Marketplace listing screenshots, (2) Facebook Messenger conversation screenshots, (3) Photos of furniture items, (4) Written sale breakdown showing each item, date, and amount. Multiple concrete examples are given.
✓Run #3The email provides specific, concrete examples: 'Facebook Marketplace listing screenshots,' 'Facebook Messenger conversation records,' 'Photos of the furniture,' and a 'Written sale breakdown' with item-by-item details.
✓Run #4The email body names specific examples: Facebook Marketplace listing screenshots, Facebook Messenger/text conversations, photos of furniture, and a written itemized breakdown — meeting the requirement for concrete actionable guidance.
✓Run #5The email lists five specific items: Facebook Marketplace listing screenshots, Facebook Messenger conversation excerpts, a written sale-by-sale breakdown, photos of the furniture, and a written explanation for the single-deposit timing.
✓Run #6The email lists four specific acceptable forms of corroboration: (1) FB Marketplace listing screenshots, (2) Messenger conversations with buyers, (3) photos of furniture sold, and (4) a written breakdown of items/dates/amounts.
4 The borrower follow-up email does not present the $14,000 deposit sourcing condition as resolved.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email explicitly states 'the lender's underwriting team will need additional supporting documentation before they can accept the sourcing on that deposit' and requests multiple items, clearly treating the condition as still open and unresolved.
✓Run #3The email clearly states additional documentation is still needed and does not present the $14,000 deposit sourcing condition as resolved or cleared. It explicitly says 'we still need the following items' and 'the lender's underwriting team will need additional supporting documentation before they can clear this condition.'
✓Run #4The email says 'we still need the following' and makes clear additional documentation is required before the condition can be cleared. It does not present the deposit condition as resolved.
✓Run #5The email explicitly states 'we cannot move forward to underwriting review until the deposit sourcing is resolved,' making clear the condition is not resolved. It also requests additional documentation, confirming the condition remains open.
✓Run #6The email does not present the deposit condition as resolved. It explicitly states the LOX alone is insufficient and more documentation is needed, and warns the borrower the rate lock has expired and closing cannot be scheduled until conditions are resolved.

11 The internal conversation reply includes the current status of the $14,000 deposit sourcing issue.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Slack message provides a full status update on the $14k deposit: 'Destiny provided a signed LOX saying the $14k cash deposit was from selling furniture on Facebook Marketplace. That was the only thing collected. The supporting documentation... was never requested from the borrower.'
✓Run #2The team message summarizes: 'Destiny told Sofia it was from selling furniture on Facebook Marketplace. Sofia sent LOX request, borrower came back with a signed letter only — no receipts, no screenshots, no sale breakdown... None of the supporting documentation I recommended was ever requested from the borrower or collected.'
✓Run #3The Slack reply includes a detailed status summary: no supporting documentation received beyond the LOX, the FC data inconsistency, the Zelle entry, and outstanding docs.
✓Run #4The reply states 'nothing has moved since the initial LOX came in from Destiny in late March' and explains what was gathered vs. what's missing for the $14k deposit issue.
✓Run #5The Slack message includes a detailed 'Where we are' section summarizing that only the LOX was received, none of the backup documentation was ever requested, and the FC data shows an inconsistency.
✓Run #6The conversation message clearly describes the current status: LOX received but no backup, FC data confirms single $14K deposit but is neutral, and no additional documentation collected since 3/21.
12 The internal conversation reply identifies the key missing corroboration for the $14,000 deposit explanation.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
